next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 10

The morning after Vel told Kem about the threshold, she handed him Maren's journal because her ribs would not let her climb the shale cut with one arm wrapped across her left side and the other guarding the book. That alone changed the road.

He caught it against his palm with a startled grab that almost made Dren laugh. The cover was dark with years of thumb oil and rain, the corners repaired twice, once badly and once by someone patient enough to make the bad repair hold. Kem looked down at it as if she had passed him something alive.

"Don't bend the spine," Vel said. She was already turning sideways to take the slope, boots testing the loose stone before she trusted it. "And if you lose it, I'll spend the last twenty-two breaking your neck."

Dren snorted and shifted his pack higher with his good arm. "There. That's affection."

Kem opened to the page she had marked at dawn. Maren's handwriting ran slanted and impatient along the top margin. Vel's newer notes sat underneath in smaller blockier script, water marks and trader names and dates squeezed around the older writing without crossing it out.

"These circles?" he asked.

"Clean wells."

"The double slash."

"Washed-out road."

He looked again. "The underline twice."

Vel braced one hand against the shale when the left side of her chest bit hard enough to blur the light for a second. "Ground good enough to sleep on."

He nodded once and kept reading as he came after her, one hand on the rock, careful now with the journal. Four days earlier he would have kept asking until he had either pulled the whole...
open_hooks_to_carry: Kem performing field medicine badly but sincerely — ugly stitches, shaking hands
Kem at 178: the arithmetic of depletion becoming personal
Vel's partial disclosure: zero is a threshold, not a death; something exists beyond it
The sub-Ledger as concept — a world without the Ledger
prev_tail_excerpt:
He moved in at once, knees in the leaf litter, needle held with far too much care. His hands were steady for about two seconds and then started to shake.

"That's not encouraging," Dren said.

"You are bleeding on me," Kem said. "I think I am allowed a moment."

"You had a moment."

"I had many moments. None of them involved this."

Vel took the waxed thread back long enough to knot it and pass the needle through the first loop. Even that small motion asked too much from her side. She gave up before the knot was perfect and handed it back.

"You're closing skin, not tying off a grain sack," she said. "Straight through. Both sides, same depth. Pull it snug, not tight. If you strangle the flesh, it tears worse later."

Kem nodded too many times.

"If I do it wrong-"

"Then you do the next one less wrong."

He looked at Dren's arm as if waiting for it to turn into something a library had prepared him for. It stayed an arm split open by silo stone.

Behind him, the last light through the birches had started to thin. Vel thought of the field north of them: Sable's notebook, Marris's better aim, Kess hearing the creek in the wrong direction, Tarrell bleeding on his records, Lenn learning not to throw at silos because he had already done it once.

No one mentioned the western break again.

Dren held his torn forearm out without ceremony. "Do it," he said.

Kem set the point of the needle to skin.
state_deltas_required: The weight of diminishing numbers; the intimacy of care given by someone who has never cared for anyone
Kem develops non-ability competence; the sub-Ledger's existence is revealed; the alliance deepens through shared vulnerability
